epics.title: Service Contract Management
epics.description: Rental/service contracts for photocopier machines — create, track, renew, cancel. A contract links a debtor to one or more service items plus spare parts with a validity period.

stories.title: Browse all service contracts
stories.i_want: to see all active service contracts with debtor and expiry info
stories.so_that: I can identify contracts expiring soon and take renewal action
acceptance_criteria: Grid shows ContractCode, Type, DebtorCode, DebtorName, StartDate, ExpiryDate, Value, Currency, Status
Filter by Type / Inactive / Done via toolbar
Double-click row opens ServiceContract_Form in edit mode
Toolbar: New, Edit, Delete, Refresh, Close — gated by access rights

stories.title: Create a new service contract
stories.i_want: to create a new rental or service contract with the debtor's machine list and spare parts allotment
stories.so_that: billing and meter readings can be tracked against the contract
acceptance_criteria: Header fields: ContractCode, Type, StartDate, ExpiryDate, Debtor (with address auto-fill), Term, Currency, Staff, Area, Branch, Remark1..4
Service Items tab: grid of linked ServiceItem codes (add/remove rows)
Spare Parts tab: grid of ItemStockCode + Qty + UnitPrice + Amount with tax calculations
Save writes header + details atomically; validation: code unique, debtor must exist in core Debtor

stories.title: Inquiry: contracts by debtor/type/date range
stories.i_want: to filter contracts by debtor, type, date range, and status
stories.so_that: I can answer customer queries and produce ad-hoc reports
acceptance_criteria: Filter panel: Debtor, Type, StartDate range, Status
Grid shows filtered rows
Export to Excel

stories.title: Outstanding contract items — machines still under contract
stories.i_want: to see every machine covered by an active (non-done non-inactive) contract
stories.so_that: I know our support footprint
acceptance_criteria: Grid joins ServiceContract × ServiceContractSVI × ServiceItem × Debtor
Only active rows shown

epics.title: Service Item Management
epics.description: Each photocopier machine under contract is a ServiceItem. Tracks the machine lifecycle: debtor ownership, meter types installed, preventive maintenance schedule.

stories.title: Browse all service items
stories.i_want: to see every machine with its current debtor, serial, and contract link
stories.so_that: I can look up any machine before dispatching
acceptance_criteria: Grid shows ItemCode, StockCode, Description, Grade, DebtorCode, DebtorName, ContractNo, ExpiryDate, PMActive

stories.title: Create/edit a service item
stories.i_want: to register a new machine with its debtor ownership, meter types, and PM schedule
stories.so_that: subsequent service notes and meter reads can be tracked
acceptance_criteria: Header: Code, StockCode, Grade, PurchaseDate, UnitPrice, Debtor, Area, Branch, PM fields (IntervalType, Value, StartDate, Active)
Meter Types tab: grid of MeterType + MinimumCharges + ChargesRate + MultiPrice
Save validates ItemCode uniqueness + Grade FK

stories.title: Reset machine debtor ownership (bulk transfer)
stories.i_want: to bulk-update the debtor ownership of multiple service items
stories.so_that: when a customer merges or sells machines, the records stay accurate
acceptance_criteria: Select source debtor → select target debtor → select items to transfer → confirm → writes history row

stories.title: Generate service items from sales invoice serial numbers
stories.i_want: to auto-create ServiceItem records from SI serial numbers when a machine is sold
stories.so_that: I don't have to type each machine manually after a delivery
acceptance_criteria: Pick debtor → pick sales invoice → select serials → generate ServiceItem rows

stories.title: Search machines by tag / remark
stories.i_want: to find machines by a custom remark or tag field
stories.so_that: I can answer obscure lookup queries fast
acceptance_criteria: Free-text search across Remark1..4 fields

epics.title: Service Note Workflow
epics.description: ServiceNote is a service job ticket — problem logged, assigned to a person, tracked to closure, and eventually invoiced.

stories.title: Log a new service note (full entry)
stories.i_want: to log an incoming customer problem with machine, problem code, severity, and assigned person
stories.so_that: it enters the service workflow for attention
acceptance_criteria: Pick ServiceItem → debtor auto-populates
Pick ProblemCode, SeverityCode, ServiceType
Assign to ServicePerson
Status defaults to OPEN

stories.title: Quick entry for walk-in service notes
stories.i_want: a streamlined one-page form to log minimal-info service notes
stories.so_that: front-desk intake is fast
acceptance_criteria: Minimal fields: Debtor, Machine, Problem, Severity
Save + New workflow

stories.title: Assign outstanding service notes to a service person
stories.i_want: a dialog listing all unassigned / open service notes with bulk-assign to a person
stories.so_that: I can distribute the day's work
acceptance_criteria: Pick ServicePerson → see list of open notes → select multiple → assign

stories.title: Close a completed service note
stories.i_want: to mark a service note as closed with solution, actual service date, and remarks
stories.so_that: the note exits the open queue and is ready for invoicing
acceptance_criteria: Pick SolutionCode, set ClosedDate, set ActualServiceDate
Closed='Y' after save

stories.title: Inquiry on service notes
stories.i_want: to filter notes by status, date range, severity, service person
stories.so_that: I can measure SLA performance and backlog
acceptance_criteria: Filter panel with date range + status + severity + person
Export to Excel

stories.title: Outstanding service note assignment report
stories.i_want: a view of notes still not closed, grouped by assigned person
stories.so_that: I can follow up on backlog
acceptance_criteria: Group by AssignToServicePersonCode
Show overdue counts

epics.title: Service Appointments & Preventive Maintenance
epics.description: Calendar view of appointments — service visits, PM, collections. Integrates with service notes and sales lead module.

stories.title: View appointment calendar
stories.i_want: a calendar showing all scheduled appointments
stories.so_that: I can see workload and schedule new visits
acceptance_criteria: DevExpress SchedulerControl
Filter by ServicePerson
Color by priority

stories.title: Create/edit an appointment
stories.i_want: to create an appointment linked to a debtor / service note
stories.so_that: the calendar reflects the planned visit
acceptance_criteria: Start/Finish pickers
Type/Priority lookups
Link to ServiceNote or SalesLead

stories.title: Preventive maintenance bulk scheduler
stories.i_want: to generate PM appointments for all machines due this week
stories.so_that: I don't miss scheduled maintenance
acceptance_criteria: List machines where PMActive='Y' and PMLastServiceDate+Interval < today
Generate appointments + service notes in bulk

stories.title: Meter type transaction entry (meter reads)
stories.i_want: to enter meter readings against a ServiceItem+MeterType so billing can calculate charges
stories.so_that: meter contracts can be invoiced
acceptance_criteria: Pick ServiceItem → show its installed meter types → enter current readings → save trans rows

epics.title: Service Module Master Setup
epics.description: All the small code-description lookup tables that drive dropdowns in the transaction forms.

stories.title: Maintain service status lookup
stories.i_want: to add/edit/disable service status values
stories.so_that: status dropdowns reflect our workflow
acceptance_criteria: Grid with Code, Description, Inactive
Edit dialog for single row
Default seed: OPEN, IN-PROGRESS, PENDING, CLOSED, CANCELLED

stories.title: Maintain service severity / solution / problem / type / contract-type / item-grade / appointment-type / appointment-priority lookups
stories.i_want: the 8 other small lookups to share the same list+edit pattern
stories.so_that: maintenance is uniform and cheap
acceptance_criteria: All 8 forms inherit from ScpLookupLst_Form + ScpLookupEdt_Form base classes
No duplicated hand-written UI per the form standardization rule

stories.title: Maintain service people (ServicePerson / ServiceAdvisor / Mechanic)
stories.i_want: to maintain the three categories of service staff
stories.so_that: each transaction can reference the right person
acceptance_criteria: Fields: Code, Name, Gender, Phone, Email, IC, Occupation, DateJoined, Inactive
All three share ScpPersonEdt_Form base

stories.title: Maintain meter types and multi-pricing
stories.i_want: to define meter types (B&W, Color, etc.) and their tiered pricing schemes
stories.so_that: meter billing charges are computed automatically
acceptance_criteria: MeterType has StockCode, MinCharges, ChargesRate
MultiPricing has tiered price breakpoints

stories.title: Global service option configuration
stories.i_want: a tabbed config dialog for module-wide settings
stories.so_that: I can toggle feature flags without code changes
acceptance_criteria: Tabs for Main, Service Note Central, etc.
Persists to z_SysConfig

epics.title: Cross-module Inquiries & Quick View
epics.description: Dashboard + aggregate views that span multiple tables for management visibility.

stories.title: Service quick view dashboard
stories.i_want: a single-screen dashboard of today's / tomorrow's appointments, open service notes, and stats
stories.so_that: I start the day with situational awareness
acceptance_criteria: Cards: Today Appts, Tomorrow Appts, Open Notes, Overdue PM
Clickable to drill into each

deferred.reason: user pre-sleep deferral (Quick View still in scope as a calendar+list)
no SalesLead table in AutoCount 2.x core
no Country table in AutoCount 2.x core
no Job table; could map to Project.ProjNo later

risks.description: Form pixel-match to screenshots is Slice 2 work, not Slice 1
AutoCount UDT d_DocNo etc. verified present in AED_ATPLUGIN001 — no issue
Appointment recurrenceinfo field is VARBINARY(MAX) — DevExpress V8→2.x binary compat unknown, new records only
11 unsorted UI screenshots in UI/_unsorted/ need manual categorization
ServiceContract_Form, ServiceItem_Form, ServiceNote_Form, Appointment_Form are placeholders — full UI deferred to Slice 2
